display.purpose: Hero headline only. One per page. Never used for section titles or card headlines.

headline.purpose: Article card titles. Clickable; hover to spot.

body.purpose: Article summaries, hero lede, modal prose. Max line length 65–75ch.

label.purpose: All metadata: datelines, sources, categories, counts. The machine voice. Never for body copy.

shadows.name: tooltip
shadows.purpose: Tippy tooltip. Warm, diffuse ambient shadow.

shadows.name: chat-fab
shadows.purpose: Chat widget FAB. Stronger warm glow so the button reads above page.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Input focus. Spot-red glow at low opacity. Only use on focusable form elements.

motion.name: hero-reveal
motion.value: opacity 0→1, y 18px→0, duration 0.6s, stagger 0.12s, ease power2.out
motion.purpose: Hero section: eyebrow, headline, and lede settle into place on page load.

motion.name: card-reveal
motion.value: opacity 0→1, y 24px→0, duration 0.5s, stagger 0.08s, ease power2.out
motion.purpose: News cards: ScrollTrigger.batch() reveal as cards enter viewport (top 88%). Default visible; transition enhances.

motion.name: live-dot
motion.purpose: Live indicator pulse. Signals real-time data.

motion.name: state-change
motion.purpose: Default state transitions: color, opacity, border on hover/focus.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Used for primary actions: sign in, create account, submit. Ink background with paper text in uppercase mono.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #211b16; color: #e9e1d0; padding: 10px 16px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; border: none; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Inline / secondary actions: Log out, Sign in link, Read →. Wire label style with hover to spot.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #6b6155; padding: 0; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: #a23b2b; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Auth modal and search form inputs. Paper background, minimal radius, spot-red focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #e9e1d0; border: 1px solid #b6a98f; border-radius: 2px; padding: 10px 12px; font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; font-size: 15px; color: #211b16; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #948a79; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #a23b2b; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(162, 59, 43, 0.12); }

components.name: News Card (Clipping)
components.description: Article cards styled as newspaper clippings. Flat (no shadow), cut edges (no radius), tonal separation from page via clip background.
components.html:
AI MODELS JUN 29

Anthropic releases new model with extended context window

The latest release pushes context limits further while improving reasoning on complex multi-step tasks.

VIA TECHCRUNCH · 3 MIN READ
components.css: .ds-card { background: #f3eddf; border: 1px solid #ccc1ab; padding: 24px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; transition: background 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; } .ds-card:hover { background: #efe7d4; border-color: #b6a98f; } .ds-card-kicker { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #211b16; } .ds-kicker-dot { display: inline-block; width: 7px; height: 7px; background: #a23b2b; margin-right: 8px; } .ds-card-date { color: #6b6155; } .ds-card-headline { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 20px; line-height: 1.2; color: #211b16; margin: 0; } .ds-card-headline:hover { color: #a23b2b; cursor: pointer; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.6; color: #6b6155; margin: 0; } .ds-card-byline { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b6155; }

components.name: Ticker Badge
components.description: The ink-background 'LATEST' tab on the wire rotator. Signals real-time data feed.
components.css: .ds-ticker-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #211b16; color: #e9e1d0; padding: 0 12px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; height: 36px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-ticker-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #a23b2b; animation: ds-pulse 1.8s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.4; transform: scale(0.8); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-ticker-dot { animation: none; } }

components.name: Filter Tab
components.description: Section filter tabs styled as column tabs — text only, underline as active indicator.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; gap: 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc1ab; } .ds-tab { background: transparent; border: none; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; padding: 10px 16px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b6155; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; margin-bottom: -1px; } .ds-tab:hover { color: #211b16; } .ds-tab.ds-tab-active { border-bottom-color: #a23b2b; color: #211b16; }

components.name: Wire Label
components.description: The system's metadata voice. Applied to datelines, sources, categories, counts, and all supporting information. Never for body copy.
components.html: VIA THE VERGE · JUN 29 · 4 MIN READ
components.css: .ds-wire { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b6155; }

narrative.overview:
AI Pulse is designed as a teletype room from the 1950s — machines printing AI news without pause, fresh ink, quiet urgency. The interface does not decorate; it transmits. Every visual decision refers back to the physical act of journalism: newsprint grain on the body, hairline rules separating columns, a masthead with the authority of a broadsheet. The typographic hierarchy is the hierarchy — not color, not cards, not icons.

The system rejects the current AI-editorial cliché: warm-beige newsletters with rounded cards, gradient headings, and a SaaS softness that mistakes approachability for credibility. It also refuses the opposite trap — bare-bones, unstyled HackerNews austerity. The wire room is disciplined, not bare; crafted, not decorated.

This is a portfolio piece. The craft is the argument. Invisible polish — contrast, rhythm, motion — is wasted; make the detail legible.
keyCharacteristics: Typographic hierarchy does all the structural work; color is reserved for punctuation
Three voices: display serif (Fraunces) for authority, body serif (Newsreader) for depth, monospace (IBM Plex Mono) for the machine layer
One spot color (printing red #a23b2b) used as a live indicator, a focus state, and nothing else
Motion is editorial: headlines settle into place, cards arrive as the reader scrolls, never as a choreographed show
The newsprint grain texture prevents the paper palette from reading as AI-generated cream

rules.name: The One Spot Rule
rules.body: The printing red (#a23b2b) appears on ≤5% of any given screen. It marks liveness, focus, and action. If it appears on more than one or two elements per viewport, it has ceased to be a spot and become wallpaper.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutral Warning
rules.body: The paper palette sits in the OKLCH range that reads as AI-generated cream in 2026. The grain texture and strict typographic hierarchy prevent this reading. Do not soften the grain, reduce ink contrast, or add rounded corners and pastel accents.

rules.name: The Three-Voice Rule
rules.body: Fraunces for authority, Newsreader for depth, IBM Plex Mono for the machine layer. Do not introduce a fourth family. Each font has one register and it must not bleed into another.

rules.name: The Masthead Exception
rules.body: The nameplate (clamp(44px, 9vw, 92px)) is the only element permitted above the display ceiling. It is not a heading; it is a logotype in type.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A card is distinguished from the background by its tonal step (clip vs. paper), not by elevation. Adding a shadow to a card converts an editorial clipping into a SaaS product card, which is prohibited.

dos: Do use text-wrap: balance on all h1–h3 headings.
Do keep the printing red (#a23b2b) to live indicators, focus rings, hover states, and the hero italic flourish only.
Do use the warm shadow formula (rgba(33, 27, 22, ...)) for all shadows.
Do keep card radius at 0. Clippings have cut edges.
Do use IBM Plex Mono for every metadata element.
Do preserve the newsprint grain texture.
Do include prefers-reduced-motion alternatives for every GSAP animation.
Do verify contrast before choosing a muted color.
donts: Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient. Gradient text is banned.
Don't add rounded corners to cards. Anything above 2px on a card is a SaaS move.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left > 1px as a colored accent).
Don't add box shadows to cards at rest.
Don't let the spot color appear on backgrounds, section headings, or more than one or two elements per viewport.
Don't design anything that looks like TechCrunch, The Verge, or Morning Brew.
Don't use numbered section markers (01/02/03) as scaffolding.
Don't soften color contrast for elegance — darken muted text until it passes 4.5:1.
Don't use glassmorphism or backdrop-filter as decoration.
